name: Sierra de San Miguel Trail
name_original: Cerro de San Miguel — circular desde Llerena
description: Hike the moderate 5.9-mile Cerro de San Miguel circular trail from Llerena, gaining 854 feet for panoramic views of the town and region.
region_intro: Hiking around Llerena offers diverse landscapes, from the extensive plains of the Campiña Sur to the rugged mountains of the Sierra Morena. The region features an ancient agroforestry system known as dehesa, characterized by holm oaks and cork oaks, alongside Mediterranean forests. Elevations range from gentle undulations across the plains to the higher peaks of the Sierra Morena, providing varied terrain for exploration. Best hiking trails around Llerena The most popular hiking route is Sierra de San Miguel Trail, a 5.9 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete, offering panoramic views of Llerena and the surrounding region. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This route explores historical sites and provides scenic vistas across the Campiña Sur. Local hikers also love the Llerena and Regina Turdulorum Roman City Loop, a 10.3 miles (16.5 km) trail leading through the Roman city ruins and surrounding countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Llerena is defined by dehesa landscapes, Mediterranean forests, and the varied terrain of the Sierra Morena and Campiña Sur. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy historical walks to moderate mountain treks. The routes in Llerena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Llerena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking around Llerena?
A: Llerena offers a diverse range of landscapes. You'll find extensive plains of the Campiña Sur, rugged mountains of the Sierra Morena, and ancient dehesa agroforestry systems characterized by holm oaks and cork oaks. Mediterranean forests are also common, providing varied scenery for your hikes.

Q: Are there hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Llerena?
A: Yes, Llerena has trails suitable for various fitness levels, including easy options perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991585002/alcazaba-de-reina-and-regina-turdulorum-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.8 km path that explores historical sites and offers scenic views across the Campiña Sur.

Q: What are some notable historical sites or landmarks I can visit on a hike?
A: Many trails in the Llerena area combine natural beauty with historical significance. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman city of Regina Turdulorum&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991662597/llerena-and-regina-turdulorum-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llerena and Regina Turdulorum Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt;. The Alcazaba de Reina also offers impressive vistas and historical context. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326816" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Our Lady of Granada&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montemolín Castle&lt;/a&gt; are nearby points of interest.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Llerena?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991662597/llerena-and-regina-turdulorum-roman-city-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Llerena and Regina Turdulorum Roman City Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.5 km trail that takes you through Roman ruins and the surrounding countryside. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31397545" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Llerena&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 13.2 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Llerena?
A: The best time for hiking in Llerena is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, temperatures are milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, and the landscapes are vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cooler and sometimes wet, though still suitable for hiking on many days.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The diverse ecosystems around Llerena support a variety of wildlife. You might spot wild boars, foxes, rabbits, hares, and badgers. Birdwatchers can look out for great bustards, storks, and numerous cranes, especially in winter. The region is also a protected area for species like the Lesser Kestrel.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1199528953/sierra-de-san-miguel-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de San Miguel Trail&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent panoramic views of Llerena and the surrounding region. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991625083/cerro-del-monigote-from-bienvenida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Monigote from Bienvenida&lt;/a&gt; offers intermediate difficulty routes with broad vistas of Extremadura.

Q: How many hiking routes are there in total around Llerena?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes available around Llerena, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These include easy walks through historical sites and moderate treks across varied terrain.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Llerena?
A: The hiking routes in Llerena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the combination of natural beauty with historical sites, and the well-maintained paths.

Q: Are there any unique natural features to look out for?
A: Yes, the region is known for its unique dehesa landscape, an ancient agroforestry system with holm oaks and cork oaks. The Sierra Morena also features extensive forests and rugged mountains. The Mina de la Jayona, an ancient iron ore mine, is a declared Natural Monument showcasing a unique blend of geology and history, accessible via the 'Rey Jayón' trail.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of hikes in Llerena?
A: Most of the hikes around Llerena are classified as moderate, offering a good balance of challenge and accessibility. There are also several easy routes available, particularly those focusing on historical sites or gentler terrain.
tips: Surrounded by beautiful buildings with typical architecture of the area, the Plaza is the cultural and social center par excellence of the town of Llerena.

The Church of Our Lady of Granada in Llerena is one of the most emblematic monuments of the city. Its construction began in the 14th century and continued until the 18th century, resulting in a mix of architectural styles including Gothic-Mudejar, Renaissance and Baroque.
The façade combines Baroque and Mudejar elements. The lower level is Baroque, with an entrance door decorated with heraldic shields. The upper level, in the Mudejar style, features galleries of semicircular arches.
The church tower is an impressive structure that dominates the square. The first two bodies are from the original Mudejar tower from the 14th century, while the three upper bodies, in the Renaissance style, were added in the 16th century.

The Mirador del Cerro de San Miguel in Llerena offers spectacular views of the Campiña Sur and the city of Llerena itself. From this elevated point, you can also see the Alcazaba de Reina in the distance.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Cerro de San Miguel circular hike?
faqs.answer: The Cerro de San Miguel circular hike is approximately 9.44 km (5.86 miles) long and generally takes about 2 hours and 45 minutes to 3 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for the Cerro de San Miguel circular trail and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Llerena. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Llerena offers various parking options within the town. The route is easily accessible from different points in Llerena.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Cerro de San Miguel trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll find approximately 6.52 km of paved track, 726 meters of asphalt, and 625 meters of natural path. One section includes a cemented path, while the circular route continues behind the Sierra.

faqs.question: What kind of views and points of interest can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: As you ascend the Sierra San Miguel, you'll be treated to magnificent panoramic views of Llerena, including landmarks like the Reina fortress and the historic Regina theater. The trail also offers views of the wider Campiña Sur region. Notable points include the Plaza de España and the Church of Our Lady of Granada in Llerena, and the San Miguel Hill Viewpoint at the summit, where there's also an hermitage.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro de San Miguel trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered of moderate difficulty, requiring a good physical condition due to its length and an elevation gain of about 260 meters. While parts are accessible, the full circular route is best suited for hikers with some experience rather than absolute beginners or very young children.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike Cerro de San Miguel?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for hiking in Extremadura, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. Check local weather forecasts before you go.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro de San Miguel trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cerro de San Miguel trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near roads, and to carry water for them, particularly during warmer months.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Cerro de San Miguel circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail connect with any other routes or networks?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Cerro de San Miguel circular route is recognized as part of the 'Circuito Camina Extremadura 2024' and the 'Liga Ibérica de Senderismo FEDME', indicating its integration into larger regional and national hiking networks. It also includes a section of the official trail 'Calle Fuente'.

faqs.question: Where can I find refreshments or accommodation near the trail in Llerena?
faqs.answer: Llerena is a town with various amenities. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options within the town center, which is the starting and ending point of the circular route. These establishments offer places to relax and refuel before or after your hike.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of Granada
